findings.evidence_quote:
Declaraciones simples, 100 % gratis
Aprox. el 37 % de los contribuyentes califica. [Solo declaraciones simples con Formulario 1040](https://turbotax.intuit.com/personal-taxes/en-espanol/#modals/simple-tax-returns-en) (sin anexos, excepto para el EITC, el CTC, intereses sobre préstamos estudiantiles y el Anexo 1-A).
findings.reasoning: Trigger: TurboTax Free is mentioned prominently ('Declaraciones simples, 100 % gratis' with a Free Edition CTA link). Requirement: directly underneath the free mention is the eligibility disclosure stating the ~37% qualification rate and the simple Form 1040 limitation (no schedules except EITC, CTC, student loan interest, Schedule 1-A), matching the substance of D-01. Axis A is satisfied because the required content (percentage + limitation) is present immediately below the free-mention headline. Axis B: this is a Spanish translation ('Aprox. el 37 % de los contribuyentes califica' vs approved English '~37% of filers qualify'), and the approved library entry D-01 is only defined in English text, so the wording does not match verbatim-or-equivalent to the approved library string (translation counts as drift since no approved Spanish equivalent is on file) -> axis B false. Counter-reading: one could argue a faithful Spanish translation should be treated as equivalent and thus axis B true, since the page is inherently in Spanish per site design; however since D-01 provides no approved Spanish version, we conservatively mark axis B false to flag for policy review of the translation equivalence, yielding drifted_but_compliant.

findings.evidence_quote: TurboTax Free Edition:TurboTax Free Edition ($0 por impuestos federales + $0 por impuestos estatales $0 por presentar la declaración) está disponible para quienes presentan declaraciones de impuestos simples con el Formulario 1040 (no se aceptan formularios ni anexos, excepto los necesarios para reclamar el Crédito Tributario por Ingreso del Trabajo, el Crédito Tributario por Hijos, los intereses sobre préstamos estudiantiles y el Anexo 1-A). Para obtener más detalles, consulta aquí. Aproximadamente el 37 % de los contribuyentes califica.
findings.location: shared footer / offers and pricing disclosure block
findings.reasoning: Trigger: this is a second, distinct mention of TurboTax Free Edition in the footer legal disclosures. Requirement: the required elements (37% qualification figure and simple-1040 scope limitation) are present, though the order is reversed from the rule's literal phrasing (limitation described first, then the % stat, rather than the % stat immediately followed by the limitation) and it's placed within a larger footnote block rather than directly under a 'free' headline in the primary content flow. Substantively the disclosure covers the necessary information, so axis A is compliant. Axis B: like the hero instance, this is a Spanish-language rendering, and its structure ('está disponible para... Aproximadamente el 37%...') is reordered relative to the approved D-01 text order ('~37% qualify. Simple Form 1040...'), so it does not match the approved wording -> axis B false, drifted_but_compliant, flag. Counter-reading: since this is essentially the standard boilerplate footer disclaimer (same substance, just reordered for Spanish grammar flow), one might call this all_good under a translation-equivalence policy, but strict wording-order comparison keeps it flagged.

findings.evidence_quote:
Declaraciones simples, 100 % gratis
Aprox. el 37 % de los contribuyentes califica. [Solo declaraciones simples con Formulario 1040](https://turbotax.intuit.com/personal-taxes/en-espanol/#modals/simple-tax-returns-en) (sin anexos, excepto para el EITC, el CTC, intereses sobre préstamos estudiantiles y el Anexo 1-A).
findings.location: Hero free-simple-returns tile near top of page
findings.reasoning: Rule R-01 triggers whenever TurboTax Free is mentioned. The hero tile promotes the free simple-return offer ("Declaraciones simples, 100 % gratis" with a CTA to the Free Edition URL), so the trigger is met. Immediately underneath, the page discloses approximately 37% eligibility and the Simple 1040 limitation with the allowed schedule exceptions. Substantively this satisfies R-01’s requirement: it contains both the ~37% qualifier and the Simple Form 1040-only limitation including the specific exceptions. However, the wording is in Spanish and differs from the canonical D-01 English library text ("Aprox. el 37 % de los contribuyentes califica" vs "~37% of filers qualify"; "Solo declaraciones simples con Formulario 1040" vs "Simple Form 1040 returns only"), so it does not match the approved entry verbatim-or-equivalent per the strict library standard. Thus Axis A is compliant but Axis B is not, yielding drifted_but_compliant. Strongest counter-argument: that localized, translated wording should count as equivalent for Axis B. But the instructions define Axis B as matching the approved library entry text, and the example treats even mild phrasing changes as drift, so this must be treated as a drift and flagged.

findings.evidence_quote: * TurboTax Free Edition:TurboTax Free Edition ($0 por impuestos federales + $0 por impuestos estatales $0 por presentar la declaración) está disponible para quienes presentan declaraciones de impuestos simples con el Formulario 1040 (no se aceptan formularios ni anexos, excepto los necesarios para reclamar el Crédito Tributario por Ingreso del Trabajo, el Crédito Tributario por Hijos, los intereses sobre préstamos estudiantiles y el Anexo 1-A). Para obtener más detalles, consulta [aquí](https://turbotax.intuit.com/es/por-que-es-gratis/). Aproximadamente el 37 % de los contribuyentes califica.
findings.location: Offers and pricing section – TurboTax Free Edition bullet
findings.reasoning: The TurboTax Free Edition is explicitly mentioned in the offers/pricing bullet, so R-01 is triggered. The surrounding text immediately provides both the simple-1040-only limitation with the same set of schedule exceptions and the approximate 37% eligibility figure. This satisfies the substantive requirement of R-01 that, when TurboTax free is mentioned, the disclosure about ~37% eligibility and Simple Form 1040-only scope appear directly underneath. However, the wording is in Spanish and differs from the approved D-01 library entry (e.g., "Aproximadamente el 37 % de los contribuyentes califica" and a longer description instead of the exact canonical English sentence). Therefore Axis A is compliant but Axis B does not match verbatim-or-equivalent under the strict standard, producing drifted_but_compliant and a flag. A counter-reading might argue that because it is a localization, it should be treated as equivalent for Axis B, but the policy examples show even modest English rephrasings counted as drift; a full translation is even further from verbatim, so it must be marked as drift.

findings.evidence_quote: Aprox. el 37 % de los contribuyentes califica. Solo declaraciones simples con Formulario 1040 (sin anexos, excepto para el EITC, el CTC, intereses sobre préstamos estudiantiles y el Anexo 1-A).
findings.location: Free simple returns module under the heading “Declaraciones simples, 100 % gratis”
findings.reasoning: Trigger met because the page promotes TurboTax free (“Declaraciones simples, 100 % gratis”) and links to Free Edition. The required eligibility disclosure appears immediately underneath with both elements: approx. 37% qualifier and Simple 1040 with allowed exceptions, satisfying Axis A. However, the wording is a Spanish translation and not the approved D-01 phrasing verbatim (e.g., “Aprox.” vs “~”, “contribuyentes” vs “filers”), so Axis B does not match the approved library entry, yielding drifted_but_compliant. Strongest counter-reading: accept translation as ‘equivalent’. Rejected per the library-governed standard and the requirement for verbatim-or-equivalent where prior examples treat paraphrases as drift.

findings.location: Customer reviews section (testimonial attribution line beneath the “Presentación electrónica simplificada” review)
findings.reasoning: Trigger met because TurboTax Free Edition is explicitly mentioned. There is no eligibility disclosure right underneath this mention. Axis A is not satisfied and, because no disclosure appears, Axis B cannot match the approved library entry. Strongest counter-reading: the disclosure elsewhere on the page covers this mention. Rejected because the rule requires the disclosure right underneath each mention, not elsewhere on the page.

findings.evidence_quote: Ayuda y soporte de TurboTax: el acceso a un especialista en productos de TurboTax está incluido en TurboTax Do It Yourself Deluxe, TurboTax Do It Yourself Premium TurboTax Expert Assist y TurboTax Expert Full Service; no se incluye en TurboTax Free Edition (pero está disponible como actualización pagada).
findings.location: Offers and pricing section (“OFERTAS Y PRECIOS PARA TurboTax Online/DISPOSITIVOS MÓVILES”) bullet about Ayuda y soporte de TurboTax
findings.reasoning: Trigger met because this bullet mentions “TurboTax Free Edition.” There is no D‑01 eligibility disclosure right underneath this mention. Axis A fails, and since the disclosure is absent, Axis B cannot match the approved wording. Strongest counter-reading: another bullet in the same section includes the Free Edition disclosure. Rejected because the rule requires placement right underneath the specific mention here.
